insight: For France, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum afterwards, with their average change in momentum being -0.300 per minute, and they were unable to regain it in any of these instances. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually gained momentum afterwards, with an average change of +0.200 per minute, suggesting that the breaks may have given them a chance to regroup and refocus. Overall, this suggests that France's strategy going into hydration breaks may be crucial in determining their success, as they need to find a way to maintain their momentum when they are on the front foot.
